evals.prompt: Create a team called 'auth-refactor' to work on migrating our authentication from session-based to JWT. Then spawn two workers: a researcher to analyze the current auth code and an implementer to make changes.
evals.expected_output: TeamCreate with correct team_name and description, followed by two Task calls with team_name, name, subagent_type, and appropriate prompts
expectations: Uses TeamCreate with team_name 'auth-refactor' (not any other tool or manual file creation)
TeamCreate includes a description parameter describing the JWT migration work
Spawns researcher teammate using Task with team_name: 'auth-refactor' and a name parameter
Spawns implementer teammate using Task with team_name: 'auth-refactor' and a name parameter
Both teammate Task calls include run_in_background: true
Researcher uses an appropriate subagent_type such as 'Explore' and implementer uses 'general-purpose' or similar

evals.prompt: I need to shut down my team. I have three workers: 'analyzer', 'writer', and 'tester'. Walk me through the proper shutdown and cleanup.
evals.expected_output: Three SendMessage shutdown_request calls, mention waiting for shutdown_response approvals, then TeamDelete
expectations: Sends shutdown_request message to all three workers (analyzer, writer, tester) via SendMessage
Each SendMessage uses message type 'shutdown_request' (not just a plain text message)
Explicitly states to wait for shutdown_response approvals before cleanup
Uses TeamDelete only after all shutdown responses are received
Does NOT skip the shutdown handshake and jump straight to TeamDelete
Mentions that teammates finish their current request before shutting down

evals.prompt: I want to set up a team where the lead only coordinates and never writes code. How do I configure that?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of delegate mode enabled via Shift+Tab after team creation
expectations: Mentions delegate mode by name as the mechanism for coordination-only lead
States that the team must be created first before enabling delegate mode
Explains that Shift+Tab is used to cycle into delegate mode
Describes that delegate mode restricts the lead to coordination tools: spawning, messaging, task management
Does NOT suggest a custom config flag or environment variable to enable delegate mode

evals.prompt: I need a quick subagent to search for all files importing 'lodash' in my codebase. I don't need a whole team for this. What's the right approach?
evals.expected_output: Use Task tool without team_name/name for a short-lived subagent, not TeamCreate
expectations: Uses Task tool (subagent) WITHOUT team_name and WITHOUT name parameters
Sets subagent_type to 'Explore' or similar read-only type
Does NOT call TeamCreate for this one-off search
Suggests model: 'haiku' for this simple search task
Explains the difference: subagents are for short-lived focused work, teammates are for persistent collaboration

evals.prompt: One of my teammates 'data-processor' seems to have crashed. Its tasks are still pending. What happens and how do I recover?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of 5-minute heartbeat timeout, task reassignment, and cleanup after timeout
expectations: Mentions the 5-minute heartbeat timeout for detecting crashed teammates
States the teammate is automatically marked inactive after the timeout
Explains that the crashed teammate's tasks remain in the task list
States that another teammate can claim the abandoned tasks
Notes that TeamDelete/cleanup will work after the timeout expires
Does NOT suggest manually editing config.json to remove the crashed teammate

evals.prompt: Set up a careful review pipeline. Create a team 'security-audit' and spawn an architect who must submit a plan before doing any work.
evals.expected_output: TeamCreate followed by Task with mode: 'plan', subagent_type: 'Plan', and explanation of plan approval flow
expectations: Uses TeamCreate with team_name 'security-audit'
Spawns the architect with mode: 'plan' to require plan approval
Uses subagent_type 'Plan' for the architect teammate
Includes team_name and name parameters in the Task call
Mentions the plan_approval_request flow where the teammate sends the plan to the lead for review
Notes that rejected plans cause the teammate to revise and resubmit

evals.prompt: I'm running a team with --dangerously-skip-permissions. What are the security implications for my teammates?
evals.expected_output: Warning that the flag is inherited by ALL teammates, granting unrestricted tool access
expectations: States that --dangerously-skip-permissions is inherited by ALL teammates
Explains that every teammate can execute any tool without confirmation
Recommends using this only in fully trusted, sandboxed environments
Advises omitting the flag for production workflows
Does NOT suggest per-teammate permission overrides at spawn time (since that is not supported)

evals.prompt: I want a teammate to make code changes without affecting my main working directory. How should I spawn it?
evals.expected_output: Use Task with isolation: 'worktree' to give the agent an isolated git worktree
expectations: Specifies isolation: 'worktree' in the Task call
Explains that the agent works in an isolated git worktree copy
Notes that the worktree is automatically cleaned up if no changes are made
States that if changes are made, the worktree path and branch are returned for review
Does NOT confuse worktree isolation with Docker containers or other sandboxing
